rag_rules: Use AGREE contracts with declarations and expressions that are syntactically valid and typed consistently.
Use only identifiers visible in the current component scope or helpers declared in the same annex.
For stateful behavior, express history with initialized temporal recurrence and guard any previous-value access with an initialization condition.
Encode branching behavior with nested if-then-else expressions.
Use assumptions or guarantees for port relations and ranges only when the relation and bounds are explicitly provided by the case.
generic_rules: Prefer small helper equations for intermediate state or derived expressions when they improve clarity and typing.
Keep each clause tied to explicit requirement semantics and visible model symbols; omit unsupported architecture or property references.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Common AGREE forms include assume/guarantee clauses, eq helper declarations, const declarations, and assign for outputs.
Comparison results are bool; integer arithmetic and literals are int; real arithmetic and literals are real.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports or out-of-scope names in a component contract.
Do not use AADL property names or unit tokens as if they were AGREE variables.
Do not use pre(...) without explicit initialization/guarding for the first step.
irrelevant_topics: Implementation-level placement details that concern where annex content belongs in AADL structure rather than AGREE expression formation.
